justifications.ip1: All three ACs have specific failing test scenarios: AC1 covered by T1/T2/T3 (e.g. 'expect(record.customerName).toBe("[REDACTED]")'), AC2 by T4/T5 (e.g. 'expect(record).toBeNull()'), and AC3 by T6/T7/T8 with concrete assertions on action, timestamp, and triggerAgeDays fields.
justifications.ip2: The plan explicitly lists all out-of-scope items (scheduling, consent workflow, attachment redaction, configurable thresholds, Azure DPA) and no task implements any of them; all constraints including C4 compiled-in constants are fully reflected in TASK 2 implementation.
justifications.ip3: Every task names specific files ('tests/crm/transcript-retention.test.js', 'src/crm/transcript-retention.js'), concrete function names ('enforceRetention'), and test assertions with exact expected values (e.g. 'expect(record.customerName).toBe("[REDACTED]")').
justifications.ip4: TASK 1 writes all tests first with explicit RED state confirmation ('All tests FAIL with Cannot find module'), TASK 2 implements only after RED is established, and TASK 4 is a distinct refactor step — preserving strict RED-GREEN-REFACTOR order throughout.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 boundary tests at all six ages (89/90/91/364/365/366) are explicitly coded in TASK 1; REDACT_AFTER_DAYS and PURGE_AFTER_DAYS are declared as module-level compiled-in constants with a Legal comment ('HARD LEGAL CONSTANTS — DO NOT make configurable') and the DoD checklist explicitly checks they are not parameters.

notes: Exemplary plan: exhaustive boundary tests, strict TDD ordering, C4 constants enforced as compiled-in values with legal commentary, full AC traceability matrix, and clean out-of-scope boundary enforcement.
